title: Debugger and final project

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for Debugger and final project; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A Python project is completed through reproducible setup, a package and command boundary, typed contracts, deterministic tests, safe logging, debugger-guided diagnosis, and documented verification evidence.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A fresh virtual environment installs the built wheel, runs the CLI's normal and failure cases, executes tests, and records exact commands in the project README. Your goal is to deliver and explain a typed, tested, logged multi-module CLI project with reproducible setup. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Project metadata and command entry; Exceptions, files and resources; Classes and object protocols; Type hints and static checking concepts; Automated tests; Logging and diagnostics. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: A Python project is completed through reproducible setup, a package and command boundary, typed contracts, deterministic tests, safe logging, debugger-guided diagnosis, and documented verification evidence. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: A local editable install can hide packaging gaps, debugger state can change timing, platform paths vary, optional dependencies need policy, and a passing happy path is not portfolio-ready evidence. The learner must reject this documented misconception: A project is complete once it runs from the developer's source directory and produces the expected output for one sample.

includes: Use breakpoints, state inspection and tests to complete a packaged command-line application.
Deliver and explain a typed, tested, logged multi-module CLI project with reproducible setup.
excludes: A local editable install can hide packaging gaps, debugger state can change timing, platform paths vary, optional dependencies need policy, and a passing happy path is not portfolio-ready evidence.

outcomes: Deliver and explain a typed, tested, logged multi-module CLI project with reproducible setup.

examples.title: Debugger and final project: direct language case
examples.situation: A fresh virtual environment installs the built wheel, runs the CLI's normal and failure cases, executes tests, and records exact commands in the project README.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A fresh virtual environment installs the built wheel, runs the CLI's normal and failure cases, executes tests, and records exact commands in the project README.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Verify the built artifact from a fresh environment and demonstrate normal, boundary, and failure contracts with tests, safe diagnostics, and reproducible commands.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Verify the built artifact from a fresh environment and demonstrate normal, boundary, and failure contracts with tests, safe diagnostics, and reproducible commands.

examples.title: Debugger and final project: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A breakpoint finds one invalid state transition, the fix gains a regression test, and logging records safe context without leaking input secrets.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A breakpoint finds one invalid state transition, the fix gains a regression test, and logging records safe context without leaking input secrets.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: A local editable install can hide packaging gaps, debugger state can change timing, platform paths vary, optional dependencies need policy, and a passing happy path is not portfolio-ready evidence.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: A project is complete once it runs from the developer's source directory and produces the expected output for one sample.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Deliver and explain a typed, tested, logged multi-module CLI project with reproducible setup.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A project is complete once it runs from the developer's source directory and produces the expected output for one sample.
misconceptions.correction: Verify the built artifact from a fresh environment and demonstrate normal, boundary, and failure contracts with tests, safe diagnostics, and reproducible commands.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: A project is complete once it runs from the developer's source directory and produces the expected output for one sample.

analogy.example: an inspected delivery package whose interface notes, repeatable checks, safe incident log, and clean installation prove bounded quality claims can introduce Debugger and final project, after the canonical Python: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: typing, tests, logging, debugging, and project completion
limitations: annotations do not enforce runtime types, mocks can mislead, logs can leak, and local editable installs can hide packaging defects

recap.summary: You should now be able to deliver and explain a typed, tested, logged multi-module CLI project with reproducible setup. Keep this boundary in view: A local editable install can hide packaging gaps, debugger state can change timing, platform paths vary, optional dependencies need policy, and a passing happy path is not portfolio-ready evidence.
